gate: official legal-document chain and version identity
evidence: REF003-R01B (Platform v15 PDF, 42pp, docLink 8f54e...), REF003-R03B (legal-terms listing: Platform version 15 docLink 8f54e9463ad3fa8ab9c0a39c0fc25f3a.pdf, updateTime 2026-07-15)
notes: Platform Terms v15 PDF (400739B, 42pp, Last-Modified 2026-07-15) matches the official legal-terms listing entry (name Bybit-BTL-Platform-Terms-and-Conditions, version 15, docLink 8f54e9463ad3fa8ab9c0a39c0fc25f3a.pdf). API Terms v1 is a distinct document (7pp, Last-Modified 2025-09-08). Version identity established for both. PASS.

gate: API Terms applicability versus Platform Terms
evidence: REF003-R02B (APIA: 'this APIA shall apply ... in addition to our TOU'), REF003-R01B (Platform TOU)
notes: API Terms & Conditions (APIA) apply 'in addition to' the Platform Terms of Use. The two documents are distinct and cumulative. APIA governs API use; Platform TOU governs Site/Platform/data. Applicability distinguished. PASS.

evidence: REF003-R02B (APIA s5: revocable license 'to use the API'; s2.2 'access, call, command, query or request the API')
notes: API Terms grant a non-sublicensable/non-transferable/revocable license to use the API, including automated query/request. Automated public-API acquisition for market data is within the granted license. PASS (subject to G07 redistribution bar).

gate: permission for internal non-commercial raw snapshot retention
evidence: REF003-R01B/R02B (no retention grant); bybit 7-year clause is Bybit's own obligation
notes: Neither Platform Terms nor API Terms grants the user an explicit right to retain raw snapshots internally for non-commercial research. The 7-year retention clause is Bybit's own data-retention obligation, not a user permission. No explicit grant => fail-closed. FAIL-UNKNOWN.

gate: deterministic instruments-info pagination and request identity
evidence: REF003-R03B (legal-terms listing deterministic, ret_code 0); no instruments-info capture
notes: The legal-terms listing API is deterministic (ret_code 0, request identity retained). However, no instruments-info (v5/market) request/response was captured in this audit; pagination/request identity for instruments-info is unverified. FAIL-UNKNOWN.

evidence: methodology: captures stamped retrieved_at 2026-07-21; no backdating
notes: Audit commits to prospective semantics: each snapshot records known_from = retrieved_at only; knowledge is never backdated to a historical state. This is a self-imposed methodology constraint, within scope (historical transitions explicitly out of scope). PASS.

evidence: REF003-R01B (Platform: 'no commercial use of the Site or Platform'; 'scraping' prohibited); REF003-R02B (APIA s6.9 'shall not commercially exploit the APIs'; s5 'shall not repackage or resell ... Service Data')
notes: Both documents bar commercial use and redistribution of Site/Platform/data and Service Data. No redistribution or commercial-use assumption is permitted. Fail-closed. FAIL.

evidence: REF003-R01B/R02B PDFs with SHA-256 + versions + docLinks; REF003-R03B listing
notes: Immutable snapshot/version lineage is established for the two PDFs (SHA-256, version, docLink) and the legal-terms listing. But because G07 bars redistribution, authority to publish/retain-distribute the snapshots is incomplete. FAIL-PARTIAL.
